ui.title: Explore the Solar System
ui.subtitle: Tour the Sun, planets, moons and dwarf planets in 3D
ui.clickHint: Click a body to explore · drag to rotate · scroll to zoom

sun.name: Sun
sun.desc: The only star of the Solar System, holding 99.8% of its mass and producing light and heat through nuclear fusion.

mercury.name: Mercury
mercury.desc: The closest planet to the Sun, with almost no atmosphere and extreme day–night temperature swings.

venus.name: Venus
venus.desc: The hottest planet, its thick carbon-dioxide atmosphere traps heat in a runaway greenhouse effect.

earth.name: Earth
earth.desc: The only known planet with liquid water and life on its surface.

mars.name: Mars
mars.desc: The 'Red Planet', colored by iron oxide and marked by signs of ancient flowing water.

jupiter.name: Jupiter
jupiter.desc: The largest planet, famous for its Great Red Spot, a storm larger than Earth.

saturn.name: Saturn
saturn.desc: A gas giant renowned for its spectacular rings of ice and rock.

uranus.name: Uranus
uranus.desc: An ice giant that orbits tipped almost entirely on its side.

neptune.name: Neptune
neptune.desc: The most distant planet, with the strongest winds and a deep blue hue.

ceres.name: Ceres
ceres.desc: The largest object in the asteroid belt and the innermost dwarf planet.

pluto.name: Pluto
pluto.desc: The iconic dwarf planet of the Kuiper Belt, once classified as the ninth planet.

haumea.name: Haumea
haumea.desc: A Kuiper Belt dwarf planet spun into an elongated egg shape by its rapid rotation.

makemake.name: Makemake
makemake.desc: A bright, reddish dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt.

eris.name: Eris
eris.desc: A distant dwarf planet similar in size to Pluto that sparked the debate over what a planet is.

moon.name: Moon
moon.desc: Earth's only natural satellite, stabilizing its tilt and driving the tides.

phobos.name: Phobos
phobos.desc: The larger of Mars's two moons, slowly spiraling inward toward the planet.

deimos.name: Deimos
deimos.desc: Mars's smaller moon, with a smooth, dark surface.

io.name: Io
io.desc: The most volcanically active body in the Solar System.

europa.name: Europa
europa.desc: An icy moon thought to hide a subsurface ocean, a prime target in the search for life.

ganymede.name: Ganymede
ganymede.desc: The largest moon in the Solar System and the only one with its own magnetic field.

callisto.name: Callisto
callisto.desc: One of the most heavily cratered bodies, with an ancient surface.

titan.name: Titan
titan.desc: Saturn's largest moon, with a thick atmosphere and lakes of liquid methane.

enceladus.name: Enceladus
enceladus.desc: An icy moon that jets water plumes from a subsurface ocean at its south pole.

rhea.name: Rhea
rhea.desc: Saturn's second-largest moon, covered in ice.

titania.name: Titania
titania.desc: The largest moon of Uranus, with canyons and icy plains.

miranda.name: Miranda
miranda.desc: A moon of Uranus famous for giant cliffs and jumbled terrain.

triton.name: Triton
triton.desc: Neptune's largest moon, orbiting backward with nitrogen geysers.

charon.name: Charon
charon.desc: Pluto's largest moon, forming a binary system as the two orbit each other.
